page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 24 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a generous 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with 2400 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated in the top tier (top 10%) with a score of 3145.78, while the multi-core stress-ng CPU performance is strong (top 25%) at 75445.88. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512_VNNI and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). However, users should note that the CPU allocation is shared, which may introduce performance variability compared to dedicated core environments under sustained peak loads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high resource density, balancing a substantial 192.0 GB memory footprint with 2400 GB of fast NVMe storage without requiring dedicated hardware pricing premiums. This makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, large-scale caching layers, and data processing pipelines that benefit from both high-capacity RAM and rapid local storage access.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC Genoa x86_64 processor with 24 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, offering an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and 2400 GB of bundled NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark testing, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance (top 25%) via stress-ng. The CPU supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained peak workloads, the high memory-to-core ratio and substantial local NVMe storage make this configuration highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 24 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 2400 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd features 24 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 2400 GB of NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory optimized instance featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors and fast bundled NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 24-core AMD EPYC Genoa x86_64 processor
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmark results
Supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions including AVX-512 and AES
Well-suited for memory-intensive databases and data processing pipelines
